name: apt-file
title: apt-file
summary: search for files within Debian packages (command-line interface)
description:
search for files within Debian packages (command-line interface)
apt-file is a command line tool for searching files contained in packages
for the APT packaging system. You can search in which package a file is
included or list the contents of a package without installing or fetching it.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use apt-file because search for files within Debian packages (command-line interface).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use apt-file as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `apt-file [options] search pattern` as documented upstream for apt-file.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `apt-file [options] show package` as documented upstream for apt-file.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair apt-file with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `apt-file --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
